q: What is a variable in Python?
a: A variable is a name you give to a piece of data so you can use it later. You make one with the equals sign, and after that the name stands in for the value.
fs: A variable is a name pointing at a value in memory; assigning rebinds the name to whatever value you give it.
fj: Thinking the name itself holds the value forever, instead of just pointing at a value you can change anytime.

q: What is a list in Python?
a: A list holds many values in order inside square brackets. You can add to it, change items, and grab one by its position number, which starts counting at 0.
fs: A list is an ordered, changeable collection; you index it from 0 and can append or replace items in place.
fj: Forgetting indexing starts at 0, so they reach for items[1] expecting the first item instead of items[0].

q: What is a dictionary (dict) in Python?
a: A dictionary stores data as key-and-value pairs, like a real dictionary maps a word to its meaning. You look things up by the key instead of by a position number.
fs: A dict maps unique keys to values for fast lookup by name, written as {key: value} in curly braces.
fj: Treating a dict like a list and trying to grab items by a number position instead of by their key.

q: What is a function in Python?
a: A function is a named block of code you write once and can run many times. You give it inputs, it does its job, and it can hand back a result with return.
fs: A function packages reusable logic: def names it, parameters take inputs, and return sends a value back to the caller.
fj: Expecting a result without writing return; the function then quietly hands back None instead of the value.

q: What is a for loop in Python?
a: A for loop repeats a block of code once for each item in a collection, like every name in a list. It saves you from copying the same line over and over.
fs: A for loop iterates over each item in a sequence, running its indented body once per item.
fj: Forgetting to indent the body, or thinking the loop variable still holds something useful after the loop ends.
